text:
University College Hospital 

National Hospital for Neurology and Neurosurgery 

Clinic Date: 21 September 2025 

Dr A. Patel  
Riverbank Medical Centre  
48 Redfern Way, London NW3 6PT  

Dear Dr A. Patel,  

Alex Morgan, DOB: 07-09-1987, Hospital No: M914276 NHS No. 3021457789  
22 Larkspur Close, London, SE15 4QH 

Summary/ Diagnoses: History of seizures with prior head injury and migraine; mood fluctuations under community psychological support; vitamin D insufficiency on supplementation; non-smoker; reports sleep variability related to shift work. 

Medication: Lamotrigine 150 mg twice daily.  
Levetiracetam 500 mg twice daily.  
Vitamin D3 1000 IU daily.  
Omeprazole 20 mg once daily. 

I reviewed Alex in clinic today. He attended alone and brought his digital seizure log and a sleep diary. He reports that stress at work and several nights of curtailed sleep preceded his recent events. He describes brief warning symptoms of difficulty focusing and a rising internal sensation, sometimes followed by unresponsiveness for under two minutes, with tiredness afterwards. There have been no tongue bites or injuries reported. He remains independent in daily activities and continues part-time employment. 

Seizures: The digital log corroborates three to four seizures during the last three months. He also notes occasional cluster patterns linked to sleep loss, particularly after consecutive late shifts. A personalised plan to manage these patterns is documented elsewhere and has been shared with his workplace health team. There have been no emergency department attendances. 

Examination: Alert, oriented, speech fluent. Cranial nerves intact. Power full in all limbs, tone normal, coordination intact, gait steady. No focal neurological deficit. 

Investigations: Routine bloods from August were within reference ranges, including U&Es and LFTs. Vitamin D recheck pending via GP. MRI (2022) unchanged; prior EEG non-diagnostic. 

Plan:  
- Continue current anti-seizure medications without change.  
- Reinforce sleep hygiene and shift planning; he will continue to use his personalised plan and share updates with the epilepsy nurse specialist team.  
- Safety: advised ongoing avoidance of heights and open water when alone; reminded of DVLA guidance as previously discussed.  
- Arrange vitamin D level check via GP next month; copy of this letter to GP and epilepsy nurse specialist.  
- Follow-up in 6 months, sooner if event pattern escalates. 

Yours sincerely

entities.text: The digital log corroborates three to four seizures during the last three months.
three to four seizures during the last three months

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter explicitly states: 'The digital log corroborates three to four seizures during the last three months.' This provides a clear numeric range over a defined time period. No other conflicting frequency is given. Clusters are mentioned but without quantifiable rate or size, so the primary explicit frequency is 3 to 4 seizures in 3 months. Normalize to the allowed format as '3 to 4 per 3 month.'
seizure_frequency_number: The digital log corroborates three to four seizures during the last three months.
reference: three to four seizures during the last three months
